name: Birchtech Corp.
business: Birchtech Corp. is a provider of specialty activated carbon technologies for air and water purification, selling patented and proprietary technologies for mercury emissions capture to the coal-fired utility sector and developing water purification technologies for PFAS and PFOS removal.
sellsTo: coal-fired power plants in North America

exposures.sentence: We provide patented and proprietary technologies for mercury emissions capture to the coal-fired utility sector, and are developing disruptive water purification technologies with a specialization on forever chemicals such as PFAS and PFOS.
We are a provider of specialty activated carbon technologies, delivering innovative solutions for air and water purification.
We also recently announced our SEA-IX TM nuclear-grade ion exchange resin product line, marking the Company's entry into the high-purity ion exchange resin market.

We also maintain a license with respect to certain intellectual property owned by Dakin Holdings Ltd., consisting of a proprietary compound of materials engineered to treat a boiler to improve the combustion process and thereby reduce overall emissions, while improving boiler efficiency during the combustion of all types of fuels at power plants.

catalysts.event: Construction of GAC reactivation facility planned for 2026
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we plan to acquire property and construct a facility dedicated to the reactivation of GAC in support of our carbon rejuvenation initiative; however, there can be no assurance that such a facility will be developed on acceptable terms, or at all.

catalysts.event: EPA finalized repeal of 2024 MATS amendments
catalysts.sentence: On February 19, 2026, following completion of the OMB interagency review process, the EPA finalized the repeal of the 2024 amendments to the MATS Rule which returned compliance to the 2012 MATS Rule requirements.

catalysts.event: Common stock listing on NYSE American
catalysts.sentence: On February 26, 2026, our shares of common stock commenced trading on the NYSE American under the symbol BCHT and ceased being traded on the OTCQB on that date.
On February 27, 2026, we completed a public offering of 6,250,000 shares of our common stock, generating gross proceeds of $15.0 million.

catalysts.event: License agreement with utility effective September 30, 2025
catalysts.sentence: Effective as of September 30, 2025, we entered into an agreement with another utility not named as a defendant in our patent litigations, but a party to the IPR petitions filed with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (PTO).

catalysts.event: License agreement with utility effective October 15, 2025
catalysts.sentence: Effective as of October 15, 2025, the Company entered into an agreement with another utility named as a defendant in the Southern District of Iowa action.

catalysts.event: Final patent infringement judgment entered December 29, 2025
catalysts.sentence: Following resolution of all post-trial motions, the Court issued the final judgment in favor of the Company on December 29, 2025 in the total amount of $78,397,157, which amount includes pre-judgment interest.

tamClaims.claim: EPA estimates PFAS rule compliance cost of approximately $1.5 billion annually.
tamClaims.sentence: The EPA has indicated that compliance with this Rule is estimated to cost approximately $1.5 billion annually.

tamClaims.claim: $9 billion in IIJA funding dedicated to PFAS pollution in drinking water.
tamClaims.sentence: The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act has dedicated $9 billion to help communities impacted by PFAS pollution in drinking water.

tamClaims.claim: Additional $12 billion in IIJA funding for general drinking water improvements.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition, another $12 billion in Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act funding is available to communities to make general drinking water improvements, including addressing PFAS chemicals.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 400 coal-fired EGUs remain in the mercury-emissions control market.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that at December 31, 2025, there were approximately 400 coal-fired EGUs remaining in the power market which make up the large mercury-emissions control market into which we sell.
